items.q: What is hexadecimal A in denary?
items.why: Students forget hex uses A-F to represent 10-15 because we need 16 distinct symbols. A=10, B=11, C=12, D=13, E=14, F=15.

items.q: What is the time complexity of binary search?
items.misconception: O(n) - because it searches through items.
items.why: Students conflate 'fast' with O(1). Binary search halves the search space each step, giving logarithmic time - much better than linear, but not constant.

items.q: Which item is removed first from a stack?
items.why: Students confuse stacks (LIFO - like a stack of plates) with queues (FIFO - like a checkout line). The 'stack of plates' analogy makes LIFO concrete.
The answer examiners want is: TCP. “UDP” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: What does HTTPS provide that HTTP does not?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Encryption. “Compression” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: What is the default port number for HTTPS?
items.why: Exact answer: 443 port. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
